data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Graphex Group ADR ( (GRFXY) ) has shared an announcement.

On May 1, 2026, Graphex Group Limited filed a Form 6-K in the United States detailing Hong Kong disclosures tied to its upcoming annual general meeting and 2025 reporting cycle. The company has published its 2025 annual report, a circular on share repurchase and issuance mandates, director re-election proposals, proxy materials, and notices of website-based corporate communications, setting the stage for shareholder decisions on capital management, board composition, and audit arrangements at the AGM scheduled for June 29, 2026.

The AGM notice outlines ordinary resolutions to receive the 2025 audited financial statements, re-elect two executive directors and one independent non-executive director, set 2026 director pay, and reappoint Crowe (HK) CPA Limited as auditor for 2026. Special resolutions seek authority for the board to repurchase up to 10% of issued shares, issue up to 20% new shares, and extend the issuance limit by the amount of shares repurchased, while also establishing share and ADS record dates that clarify how Hong Kong shareholders and U.S. ADR holders can exercise their voting rights.

Spark’s Take on GRFXY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GRFXY is a Neutral.

Graphex Group ADR’s overall stock score is primarily impacted by its weak financial performance and poor valuation metrics. The technical analysis further indicates bearish momentum, with the stock trading below key moving averages and nearing oversold conditions. The lack of profitability and absence of a dividend yield make the stock less attractive to investors.

To see Spark’s full report on GRFXY stock, click here.

More about Graphex Group ADR

Graphex Group Limited is a Cayman Islands–incorporated company whose ordinary shares trade on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and whose American Depositary Shares trade on the OTC Expert Market. The group operates as a foreign private issuer in the United States, providing Hong Kong–standard financial reporting and corporate governance disclosures to both Hong Kong and U.S. investors.

data.title: Arvinas Gains First FDA Approval for Breast Cancer Drug
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Arvinas Holding Company ( (ARVN) ) has shared an announcement.

On May 1, 2026, Arvinas and partner Pfizer announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration approved VEPPANU (vepdegestrant) for adults with ER-positive/HER2-negative, ESR1-mutated advanced or metastatic breast cancer whose disease has progressed after at least one line of endocrine-based therapy. The approval came ahead of the June 5, 2026 PDUFA date and is based on the Phase 3 VERITAC-2 trial, in which VEPPANU significantly improved progression-free survival versus fulvestrant with mostly low-grade adverse events.

VEPPANU is the first and only FDA-approved PROTAC protein degrader, marking Arvinas’s first approved medicine and a milestone for the targeted protein degradation field, while offering a new oral option for patients facing endocrine resistance and limited second-line choices. The decision triggers a $50 million development milestone payment from Pfizer to Arvinas, net of amounts owed to Yale University under a license agreement, and the partners plan to appoint a third-party commercialization partner to maximize VEPPANU’s market potential.

The most recent analyst rating on (ARVN) stock is a Buy with a $18.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Arvinas Holding Company stock, see the ARVN Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ARVN Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ARVN is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (ongoing losses, significant cash burn, and 2025 gross profit anomaly) and bearish technical signals (below key moving averages with negative MACD). Pipeline and catalyst messaging (earnings call and ARV-102 Phase 1 update) provide support but are tempered by the sharply reduced cash position and execution/partnering risk.

To see Spark’s full report on ARVN stock, click here.

More about Arvinas Holding Company

Arvinas, Inc. is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on targeted protein degradation therapies, including its proprietary PROteolysis Targeting Chimera (PROTAC) platform. Working with partner Pfizer Inc., Arvinas develops novel oncology, neurodegenerative, and neuromuscular treatments, aiming to address diseases driven by pathogenic proteins and resistance to existing therapies.

Average Trading Volume: 827,511

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: $633.2M

data.title: Akanda Corp. Delays 20-F Amid Strategic Shift
data.description:
Akanda Corp ( (AKAN) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Akanda Corp. has filed a Form 12b-25 indicating a delay in submitting its Annual Report on Form 20-F (Yearly Report) for the financial year ended December 31, 2025. The company says it could not compile all required disclosure information in time without incurring unreasonable effort or expense, prompting the late filing notice.

Management attributes the delay primarily to the complexity of preparing its year-end disclosures following significant changes in operations and structure. These include the liquidation of its Canmart business in the U.K. and the acquisition of First Towers & Fiber Corp., which together require extensive legal, accounting and disclosure work.

Akanda Corp. has committed to filing its Form 20-F (Yearly Report) no later than the fifteenth calendar day after the original SEC deadline. Investors should therefore expect the overdue annual report to be submitted within this grace period, assuming no further complications arise.

The company has signaled a major change in its financial profile, with revenues of about $258,075 for 2025 versus zero revenue in 2024, driven by leasing fiber networks and telecom towers in Mexico. Despite higher operating expenses of roughly $4.83 million in 2025, the net loss improved to around $3.76 million from $4.10 million a year earlier, though these figures remain subject to final reporting and forward-looking uncertainties.

Akanda notes that operating expenses rose mainly due to legal, audit, accounting and consulting fees tied to SEC compliance, plus higher depreciation and amortization linked to new infrastructure assets. These details point to a business in transition, investing heavily in governance and telecom growth while still operating at a loss.

The company affirms that all other required periodic reports have been filed on time, indicating efforts to remain in good standing with U.S. securities regulators. The notification is signed by Interim Chief Executive Officer and Director Katie Field on May 1, 2026, underscoring management’s formal commitment to complete the Form 20-F (Yearly Report) and maintain regulatory compliance.

More about Akanda Corp

Akanda Corp. is an international company that has shifted its focus from cannabis operations in the U.K. to telecommunications infrastructure. The company recently liquidated its Canmart business and now generates revenue through First Towers & Fiber Corp., which develops and leases fiber optic networks and telecom towers in Mexico.

Average Trading Volume: 1,961,544

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $26.16M

data.description:

Aegon ( (AEG) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On April 30, 2026, Aegon announced it had priced USD 500 million of senior unsecured notes with a 5.625% fixed coupon and a ten-year maturity, due May 7, 2036, to be issued by Aegon Funding Company LLC and guaranteed by Aegon Ltd. The issuance, expected to settle on May 7, 2026, will primarily fund the repurchase of certain unregistered subordinated notes via a concurrent tender offer for eligible non-U.S. investors, with any remaining proceeds earmarked for general corporate purposes, and an application has been made to list the new notes on the New York Stock Exchange.

The deal underscores Aegon’s continued active management of its capital structure by replacing subordinated debt with senior funding while broadening its presence in U.S. dollar capital markets. Listing on the NYSE is set to enhance liquidity and visibility of the notes for global fixed-income investors, and the transaction may improve the company’s funding flexibility and optimize its liability profile over the coming decade.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

The latest update is out from Cboe Global Markets ( (CBOE) ).

On May 1, 2026, Cboe Global Markets, Inc., a major operator of cash and spot markets, derivatives exchanges, and market data services under its Cboe Data Vantage unit, reported record first-quarter 2026 financial results, including a 29% rise in net revenue to $728.9 million and a 54% jump in diluted EPS to $3.66. Strong growth in cash and spot markets, a 32% increase in derivatives revenues on record index options volumes, and a 19% gain in Data Vantage revenues underpinned an expansion in operating and EBITDA margins.

Alongside the earnings, Cboe advanced its strategic realignment launched after a 2025 portfolio review, raising 2026 organic net revenue and Data Vantage growth targets while cutting adjusted operating expense guidance to $838–$853 million. The company announced further restructuring measures expected to reduce its workforce by about 20% when combined with earlier asset sales and wind-downs, incurring $36–$46 million in pre-tax charges largely for severance from the second to fourth quarters of 2026, and targeting $40–$50 million in annualized pre-tax cost savings to fund investment in core franchises and emerging areas such as financial event markets, tokenization, expanded clearing, and global sales and education efforts.

Schneider National ( (SNDR) ) has issued an update.

On April 30, 2026, Schneider National’s shareholders approved an amendment and restatement of the 2017 Omnibus Incentive Plan, increasing the Class B common stock reserved for issuance under the plan by 19,900,000 shares, which expands the company’s capacity to grant equity-based awards and may further align management and employee incentives with long-term shareholder interests. data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Douglas Dynamics ( (PLOW) ) has issued an update.

On April 30, 2026, Douglas Dynamics amended its Fourth Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation in Delaware to add exculpation provisions that limit personal liability for certain officers, following stockholder approval of the change at its annual meeting on April 29, 2026. At that 2026 annual meeting, shareholders elected four directors to terms expiring in 2028 and 2029, approved the company’s executive compensation on an advisory basis, ratified Deloitte & Touche LLP as independent auditor for 2026, and backed the officer exculpation amendment, signaling broad investor support for the board’s governance and risk-allocation framework.

These governance changes may strengthen Douglas Dynamics’ ability to attract and retain senior executives by narrowing certain legal exposures while keeping oversight with shareholders through regular director elections. The strong affirmative vote on pay practices and auditor ratification further reinforces continuity in the company’s leadership, compensation design, and financial reporting oversight, which are key concerns for institutional investors and other long-term stakeholders.

data.title: RREEF Property Trust Announces April 2026 Monthly Distributions
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

RREEF Property Trust, Inc. Class A ( (ZRPTAX) ) has provided an announcement.

RREEF Property Trust, Inc. declared monthly distributions on May 1, 2026 for all classes of its common stock for the month of April 2026, with payments scheduled for May 4, 2026 to stockholders of record as of May 1, 2026. The company set a uniform gross distribution of $0.07495 per share across all classes, but net distributions vary by share class due to differing dealer manager and distribution fees, resulting in higher net payouts for classes without such fees.

Class A investors will receive a net distribution of $0.06380 per share, while Class I shareholders will get $0.06928 and Class T and Class T2 will receive $0.06211 and $0.06562 respectively, reflecting their specific fee structures. Holders of Classes D, M-I, N, and Z, which carry no dealer manager or distribution fees, will receive the full $0.07495 per share, underscoring how share class selection directly affects investor income from the trust’s distributions.

More about RREEF Property Trust, Inc. Class A

RREEF Property Trust, Inc. is a real estate investment trust that offers multiple classes of common stock, providing individual and institutional investors with access to diversified real estate investment strategies. The company structures share classes with varying fee arrangements, influencing net distributions received by different categories of shareholders.

IES Holdings ( (IESC) ) has shared an announcement.

IES Holdings reported strong results for its fiscal second quarter ended March 31, 2026, with revenue up 17% year over year to $974 million and operating income up 21% to $112.3 million. Net income jumped 56% to $109.9 million, while remaining performance obligations reached about $2.3 billion and backlog climbed to roughly $3.9 billion, reflecting accelerating customer demand.

Growth was driven by robust performance in the Communications and Infrastructure Solutions segments, particularly in data center and industrial markets, while the Residential business weakened on soft housing starts and pricing pressure. The January 2026 acquisition of Gulf Island Fabrication boosted Infrastructure Solutions revenue and underpins a strategy of prioritizing capacity expansion and organic growth, with management significantly increasing capital spending plans despite expecting Gulf Island’s earnings contribution to be more meaningful in fiscal 2027.

The Commercial & Industrial segment delivered modest revenue growth but significantly higher operating income on strong project execution and data center demand. During the quarter, IES deployed capital toward the Gulf Island purchase, capex, marketable securities, and share repurchases, yet finished with net cash and sizable liquidity, positioning the company to support its expanding backlog and multi-year growth initiatives.

data.title: Colgate-Palmolive Expands Strategic Growth and Restructuring Program
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Colgate-Palmolive ( (CL) ).

On July 31, 2025, Colgate-Palmolive’s board approved a three-year Strategic Growth and Productivity Program aimed at aligning its organizational structure with its 2030 strategy, optimizing its global supply chain and reducing overhead costs. Building on progress to date, the board expanded the program on April 30, 2026, significantly increasing its scope and associated restructuring charges.

The program is now expected to generate cumulative pre-tax charges of $350 million to $550 million, up from a prior estimate of $200 million to $300 million, with 70% to 80% tied to severance and other employee-related costs and 20% to 30% to asset write-offs, accelerated depreciation and contract exits. The charges, 80% to 90% of which will require cash outlays, are expected to be largely incurred by December 31, 2028, with impacts spread across all major regions, Hill’s Pet Nutrition and corporate functions, signaling a broad-based reshaping of Colgate-Palmolive’s operations to underpin future growth and margin improvement.

The most recent analyst rating on (CL) stock is a Hold with a $79.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Colgate-Palmolive stock, see the CL Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CL Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CL is a Neutral.

The score is driven mainly by strong cash-flow generation and a durable revenue base, partially offset by elevated balance-sheet risk (high leverage vs. very thin equity). Technical indicators are weak and weigh on the rating, while valuation is mixed (reasonable yield but negative P/E). The earnings call supports steady execution (Hill’s/emerging markets strength) but highlights meaningful near-term uncertainty and a wide guidance range.

data.title: NPK International Highlights Growth in May 2026 Presentation
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from NPK International ( (NPKI) ).

NPK International Inc. operates as a specialty rental and services company focused on temporary worksite access solutions for power and infrastructure markets. It manufactures, sells, and rents recyclable composite matting products, led by its DURA-BASE advanced-composite mat system, and supports customers with planning, logistics, installation, and site restoration across a global footprint.

The company is vertically integrated with engineering, manufacturing in Carencro, La., and a large rental fleet of about 220,000 composite mats serving diverse end-markets, including power transmission, oil and gas, pipeline, infrastructure construction, and rail. It reported trailing 12‑month revenue through the first quarter of 2026 of $287 million and adjusted EBITDA of $78 million, with approximately 69% of revenue from rental and services and 31% from product sales.

NPK’s May 2026 investor presentation highlighted continued growth, with trailing 12‑month revenue up 23% year over year and adjusted EBITDA up 26%, translating into an adjusted EBITDA margin of 27.3% and modest net debt of $4 million as of March 31, 2026. Management attributed performance to strong demand for its composite matting solutions, increased production capacity, and a flexible model that offers both rental and purchase options, positioning the company to benefit from sustained investment in power transmission and other infrastructure projects.

The company emphasized the scale of its U.S. composite mat manufacturing and rental fleet, ongoing capacity expansion, and focus on safety, environmental performance, and lifecycle cost advantages as key differentiators in its markets. Its end‑market mix and the regulated, long‑term nature of power transmission investments provide a stable demand backdrop, while non‑GAAP metrics such as adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow are used internally and by investors to assess operating performance and capital structure.

The most recent analyst rating on (NPKI) stock is a Buy with a $18.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on NPK International stock, see the NPKI Stock Forecast page.
